title: Print vector elements with product prices

content.problemStatement:
In this example, we demonstrate how to access and print individual elements from a vector in C++ using both the square bracket `[]` method and the `.at()` method.

We use a vector to store product prices and then access specific elements using their indices.

---

**When executed, the code will display:**
```java
Displaying product prices at specific indices using []:
19.5 12.99 27.45
Displaying product prices at specific indices using .at():
19.5 12.99 27.45
```
